### Post by insub2 on 2005-12-17
i really want more screen space on my 17" monitor. when i had winXP i had it larger (1152x864?) but the options in screen resolution stop at 1024x768.  how can i change this?

i looked around the forums and figured you'd need the following.

did:
```
$ sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
got:
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"GLcore"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NV31 [GeForce FX 5600]"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NV31 [GeForce FX 5600]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by infoseeker on 2005-12-17
Here is a section of mine ... simply edit it.
> 	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"

### Post by afhp on 2005-12-17
[QUOTE=insub2]i really want more screen space on my 17" monitor. when i had winXP i had it larger (1152x864?) but the options in screen resolution stop at 1024x768.  how can i change this?

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NV31 [GeForce FX 5600]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		**Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"**
	EndSubSection
(... 8< ...)
EndSection

```[/QUOTE]

Two things : 
a) in the "Monitor" section, make sure HorizSync and VertRefresh actually reflect what your monitor can do (look up its manual).
b) In the "Screen" section, all your "Modes" lines (for every depth) top up at 1024x768, that's where you'll want to indicate the additional modes.

What the display will actually do depends on a combination of those "Modes", video card memory and monitor refresh rates : IOW you can indicate resolutions here that won't be available because they're not physically possible for your hardware. 

The resolutions under Modes are listed in the order in which they're tried, and cycled through by X when you use CtrlAlt+ and CtrlAlt-.

Easiest way to edit the file (backup first!!) is through
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

---

### Post by insub2 on 2005-12-17
how do i back it up?

and if i mess up how do i return to the backup?


also does it matter that my moniter came with a disk.

---

### Post by Gustav on 2005-12-17
Back it up - sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
Restore backup -  sudo mv /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup /etc/X11/xorg.conf

For a more complicated way (that works better for me at least) look at this [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=83973](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=83973)

---

### Post by bscbrit on 2005-12-17
It shouldn't matter that your monitor came with its own driver disk or CD.  In fact, most of them contain 'useful' utilities that will not run under linux anyway.  You system has recognised the screen - you are already using it - all it does not know are the resolutions that can be used.  A previous post has already identified the problem in your xorg.conf.

On the each of the lines of xorg.conf that show:

modes "1024x768" .....

change the lines to read

modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" .....

and then restart X.  You can do this by logging off or, more quickly, by CTL-ALT-Backspace.  

It starts up using the resolution that is first on the line i.e. in this case "1280x1024".

Don't forget to make a backup in case you are asking you monitor to do something that it cannot achieve.  Most 17" LCD can do 1280x1024 but CRT monitors vary considerably.

---

### Post by insub2 on 2005-12-17
thank you all for your help
but there was a little more to it.
my monitor was not autodetected properly so i had to edit that code too.

```
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	**"Generic Monitor"**
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	**28-51**
	VertRefresh	**43-60**
EndSection
```
CHANGED TO
```
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	**31.47-80**
	VertRefresh	**56.25-75**
-EndSection
```

found those numbers in the manual


thanks again.
